summary: Ukraine reports increased Shahed drone activity across multiple oblasts and a recent Russian ballistic missile strike on Zaporizhzhia, while Russian officials claim the ability to launch up to 100 ballistic missiles per month. Ukrainian intelligence highlights continued UAV incursions in Kharkiv, Sumy, and Dnipropetrovsk regions. Diplomatic efforts continue, with Zelensky's open letter to Putin and EU funding movements for Ukrainian defense.
key_points: Russian forces launched a missile strike on Zaporizhzhia, injuring 10 people and damaging a residential high‑rise building. (Source: @UkraineNow/81681)
Ukrainian air defence radar reports multiple Shahed drones targeting areas in Chernihiv, Poltava, Kyiv, and Kherson regions. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67485)

Ukrainian intelligence (HUR) states Russia can employ up to 100 ballistic missiles monthly, with production rates of 55‑60 units and plans for up to 60 Kinzhal hypersonic missiles this year. (Source: @UkraineNow/81671)
Numerous UAV sightings reported: BPLAs over Kharkiv near Bohodukhiv, Sumy from the north, and Dnipropetrovsk near Vasylkivka. (Source: @kpszsu/64044, @kpszsu/64040)
Hungary lifted a €6.6 billion EU fund veto for Ukrainian weapons purchases, according to Hungarian media. (Source: @tass_agency/379216)
Zelensky sent an open letter to Putin proposing a meeting and a full ceasefire, citing monthly Russian casualties of over 30 000. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23550)
US lawmakers passed a resolution urging Trump to end the war in Iran, while Israel and Lebanon announced a ceasefire contingent on Hezbollah's withdrawal. (Source: @UkraineNow/81656)

sections.executive_summary: Russian missile strike on Zaporizhzhia injures civilians and damages infrastructure, highlighting ongoing high‑intensity attacks. Ukrainian air defence reports a surge in Shahed drone incursions across several front‑line oblasts, indicating intensified Russian aerial pressure. Diplomatic channels remain active, with Zelensky's open letter to Putin and EU financial support for Ukrainian defence.

points: Zelensky's open letter to Putin proposes a meeting, a full ceasefire, and prisoner exchange, emphasizing monthly Russian casualties of over 30 000 and urging an end to hostilities. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23550)

Ukraine's HUR intelligence assesses Russia's capacity to launch up to 100 ballistic missiles per month, maintaining steady production of 55‑60 missiles and planning delivery of up to 60 Kinzhal hypersonic missiles this year. (Source: @UkraineNow/81671)

Ukrainian forces report multiple Shahed drone attacks: three in Chernihiv heading toward Kyiv, two along the Poltava‑Chernihiv border, and one near Kirovohrad heading to Dnipropetrovsk. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67485)
Ukrainian air defence radar logs UAV activity over Kharkiv near Bohodukhiv moving southwest, and over Sumy from the north, indicating continued Russian aerial reconnaissance. (Source: @kpszsu/64044)
A logistics centre of Nova Poshta in Dnipro was attacked for the second consecutive day, with assessments of damage ongoing and cargo losses being evaluated. (Source: @UkraineNow/81676)

Russian officials claim the ability to launch up to 100 ballistic missiles monthly, with production rates of 55‑60 units and plans to field up to 60 Kinzhal hypersonic missiles this year. (Source: @UkraineNow/81671)
Hungary removed its €6.6 billion EU fund veto for Ukrainian weapons procurement, facilitating further Russian‑targeted aid flows. (Source: @tass_agency/379216)
Russia's defense ministry reported no intention to close the automotive market, focusing on local production advantages over imports. (Source: @tass_agency/379220)
Russian spokesperson Peskov suggested Zelensky could travel to Moscow for talks, indicating a potential diplomatic opening. (Source: @tass_agency/379188)
UAVs observed over Kharkiv region near Bohodukhiv moving southwest, and over Sumy from the north, suggesting Russian aerial pressure on the eastern axis. (Source: @kpszsu/64044)
Multiple Shahed drones reported heading toward Kyiv and Dnipropetrovsk from the east, increasing the threat to rear areas. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67485)
Russian missile capability statements indicate sustained ballistic missile launches that could affect eastern Ukrainian cities. (Source: @UkraineNow/81671)
Shahed drones detected over Chernihiv heading from the north toward Nizhyn and Malа Dіvitsia, indicating pressure on the northern front. (Source: @eRadarrua/95457)
UAV sightings over Sumy region from the north, targeting areas near Putivl and Konotop, suggest continued Russian aerial activity in the north. (Source: @eRadarrua/95440)
Additional Shahed drones reported near Kyiv region from the north, increasing the risk to the capital's airspace. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67485)
Russian missile strike on Zaporizhzhia caused civilian injuries and damage to a high‑rise building, demonstrating ongoing southern offensive actions. (Source: @UkraineNow/81681)
Shahed drones reported over the Black Sea heading toward Chornomorsk/Limanok, with 10 drones observed, indicating maritime threat to southern ports. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67487)
UAV activity noted over Dnipropetrovsk region near Pavlograd and Samara, suggesting Russian reconnaissance in the southern industrial belt. (Source: @eRadarrua/95463)
Ukrainian air defence logs multiple Shahed drone incursions across Chernihiv, Poltava, Kyiv, and Kherson oblasts, reflecting heightened aerial threat. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67485)
UAV sightings over Kharkiv, Sumy, and Dnipropetrovsk regions indicate persistent Russian drone operations targeting both front‑line and rear areas. (Source: @kpszsu/64044, @kpszsu/64040)
Russian claims of capacity to launch up to 100 ballistic missiles per month suggest a significant strategic air‑to‑ground capability. (Source: @UkraineNow/81671)

key_developments: Russian missile strike on Zaporizhzhia injured 10 civilians and damaged a residential high‑rise building, highlighting ongoing high‑intensity attacks on southern Ukraine. (Source: @UkraineNow/81681)

Ukrainian air defence reported three Shahed drones over Chernihiv heading toward Kyiv, two along the Poltava‑Chernihiv border, and one near Kirovohrad toward Dnipropetrovsk, indicating a surge in drone attacks. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67485)

Ukraine's HUR intelligence states Russia can launch up to 100 ballistic missiles monthly, with production of 55‑60 missiles and plans for up to 60 Kinzhal hypersonic missiles this year. (Source: @UkraineNow/81671)
Hungary lifted a €6.6 billion EU fund veto for Ukrainian weapons purchases, facilitating increased military aid flow. (Source: @tass_agency/379216)
Zelensky sent an open letter to Putin proposing a meeting, a full ceasefire, and a comprehensive prisoner exchange, citing monthly Russian casualties exceeding 30 000. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23550)
Multiple Shahed drones were observed over the Black Sea heading toward Chornomorsk/Limanok, with 10 drones reported, raising concerns for southern maritime infrastructure. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67487)
Ukrainian logistics centre of Nova Poshta in Dnipro was attacked for the second day, with damage assessments and cargo loss evaluations ongoing. (Source: @UkraineNow/81676)
sections.threat_assessment: Russian aerial pressure remains high, with frequent Shahed drone incursions across northern, central, and southern fronts, and a demonstrated capacity to launch large numbers of ballistic missiles. Ukrainian air defence is actively engaging these threats, but the volume suggests a sustained Russian effort to degrade infrastructure and morale. The recent missile strike on Zaporizhzhia underscores the risk to civilian centers, while the reported production rates of ballistic and hypersonic missiles indicate Russia can maintain long‑range strike capabilities throughout 2026.
sections.regional_response: Western allies continue to bolster Ukrainian defence, exemplified by Hungary's removal of funding vetoes and ongoing diplomatic initiatives such as Zelensky's open letter to Putin. The EU is preparing further integration steps for Ukraine, while the United States supports additional aid packages. Regional actors are monitoring the escalation, with concerns about potential escalation from missile deployments.
sections.intelligence_notes: Ukrainian sources should prioritize tracking Shahed drone launch vectors from the north and south to anticipate future strikes on critical infrastructure. Monitoring ballistic missile production and launch patterns will be essential for early warning. Coordination with NATO and EU partners is vital to sustain air defence capabilities and counter the increasing volume of Russian aerial attacks.
